marketState: neutral, hot-weather bullish near-term vs storage-surplus/weak-seasonality bearish medium-term
summary: Front price ($2.923) sits exactly at modeled fair value with a wide $2.423-$3.423 band, so there is no valuation edge in either direction right now. The dominant near-term driver is weather: 1-7 day and 8-16 day demand are +20.9% and +17.8% above the 5-year norm with range positions of 0.83 and 0.91, a clearly bullish cooling-demand signal that holds through the full 16-day forecast window. That bullish pull is offset by a +180 Bcf storage surplus vs the 5-year average and a bearish build surprise (+22 Bcf vs forecast), plus daily/weekly technicals still bearish (50<200 EMA, negative MACD, RSI ~38) and managed money net short at the 35th percentile. Seasonality is flagged Low reliability this week but shows only 38% odds of higher prices in 3-4 weeks and a -1.4% typical 4-week move, tilting the medium-term case lower. Net: buy near-term weather-driven strength, fade rallies further out.

shortTerm.rationale: 1-7D and 8-16D demand are running well above 5-year norms (+20.9%/+17.8%, range position 0.83/0.91), supporting spot strength, and front trades slightly above next (backwardation) reflecting prompt tightness. Short-dated technicals (5m-4h) show positive MACD momentum despite a 1h overbought Bollinger reading, while price is fairly valued with room toward the plus7D/plus14D fair levels ($2.95-$3.00).
shortTerm.invalidation: Sustained close below $2.75, or the 8-16D weather demand forecast reverting toward/below the 5y norm.
